name: DScribe (Machine Learning Descriptors for Materials)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); AALTO University / Lauri Himanen

performance.notes: C++ backend with OpenMP; fast for typical atomic structures

agent_readiness.notes: Machine learning descriptors (fingerprints) for atomic structures. from dscribe.descriptors import SOAP, ACSF, CoulombMatrix, MBTR, LMBTR, ValleOganov. soap = SOAP(species=['C','H','O'], r_cut=6.0, n_max=8, l_max=6, sigma=0.1). soap_vectors = soap.create(atoms). soap.create_single(atoms). ACSF: symmetry functions for neural network potentials. CoulombMatrix: eigenvalue features. MBTR: many-body tensor representation. sparse=True for large systems. positions=[indices] for local descriptors. ASE Atoms objects as input.

gotchas: Requires ASE Atoms objects — not compatible with RDKit/pymatgen directly
SOAP hyperparameters (r_cut, n_max, l_max) require careful tuning
Large systems with sparse=False can run out of memory
Different descriptors have very different memory requirements
best_when: Computing SOAP, ACSF, or MBTR descriptors for ML potential training
Materials property prediction with structure-based features
When symmetry-adapted features are needed over matminer's Magpie
avoid_when: When graph neural network approaches are preferred (use SchNet/MACE)
summary: DScribe computes symmetry-adapted ML descriptors for atomic structures. SOAP(species, r_cut, n_max, l_max).create(atoms). ACSF, CoulombMatrix, MBTR. sparse=True for memory efficiency. Local descriptors via positions=[indices]. Apache-2.0 by Aalto/Lauri Himanen. Best for SOAP/ACSF feature generation for ML interatomic potentials. Requires ASE Atoms. Hyperparameter tuning needed.
